channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: U.S. Reports Suggest Iranian Government Remains Stable Despite Ongoing Conflict
item.description: Recent U.S. intelligence assessments suggest that the Iranian government remains stable and is not facing imminent collapse, even after nearly two weeks of sustained U.S. and Israeli military operatio...

item.title: Miguel Cardoso criticises PSL for congested schedule ahead of Sundowns’ CAF quarterfinal
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns coach Miguel Cardoso has voiced strong concerns over the Premier Soccer League's scheduling, which he says hampers the club’s preparation for their CAF Champions League quarterfinal ...

item.title: South Africans in UAE teaching and hospitality sectors stay despite regional tensions
item.description: Many South Africans working in the UAE, particularly in teaching and hospitality, are choosing to stay despite the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The Department of International Relations and Co...

item.title: Andy Carroll reduces asking price to finalise divorce and move on with new relationship
item.description: Andy Carroll is seeking to sell the Essex mansion he once shared with his ex-wife, Billi Mucklow, in a bid to finalise their divorce and move forward with his new partner, celebrity hair stylist Lou T...

item.title: Iraq Suspends Oil Terminals After Attacks Amid Rising Tensions in Gulf Region
item.description: Recent developments in West Asia have escalated tensions and disrupted global energy supply. Iraq has suspended operations at all its oil terminals following attacks on two oil tankers near Basra, whi...

item.title: ROTC students intervene to stop school shooting at Old Dominion University
item.description: In a dramatic incident at Old Dominion University in Virginia, a shooter entered the campus, leaving one person dead and two others injured. The situation was quickly brought under control by ROTC stu...

item.title: Opposition halts FC upgrade bill due to lack of quorum in National Assembly
item.description: On Thursday, the opposition in Pakistan's National Assembly stopped the passage of a bill intended to transform the Frontier Constabulary (FC) into the Federal Constabulary, citing absence of quorum. ...

item.title: US Embassy Issues Security Advisory for American Citizens in Nigeria
item.description: The U.S. Embassy for Abuja don release fresh security alert sey e get possible terrorist threat wey fit target American facilities and U.S.-affiliated schools for Nigeria. The advisory wey dem post fo...

item.title: HakiRasilimali Report Highlights Strengths and Gaps in Responsible Mining at Barrick's Bulyanhulu, Company Disputes Key Claims
item.description: Ripoti mpya ya utafiti iliyotayarishwa na shirika la kiraia HakiRasilimali imetoa tathmini mchanganyiko wa sekta ya uchukuzi madini nchini Tanzania. Ripoti hiyo inatumia viwango vya kimataifa vya Towa...

item.title: Comelec adopts energy conservation measures to reduce fuel and electricity costs
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has rolled out a new Energy Conservation Protocol (ECP) to address the rising cost of fuel and promote energy efficiency across its offices. The ECP, released on ...

item.title: Analysing the Current Dynamics and Challenges of the Iran Conflict
item.description: Iran's newly appointed supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, has signalled no intention of ending resistance, stating that Iran will open new fronts in the conflict and continue to block the Strait of Hor...

item.title: The Growing Threat of GPS Disruption to Commercial Shipping and Maritime Safety
item.description: The conflict in Iran has exposed a critical vulnerability in maritime navigation: GPS disruption. Modern ships rely heavily on satellite navigation, which can be manipulated or blocked, leading vessel...

item.title: Environmental authorisation of Nuclear-1 power station challenged in court
item.description: Three environmental justice organisations—Safcei, Greenpeace Africa, and Earthlife Africa Johannesburg—have taken Eskom to the High Court over the environmental authorisation for the proposed Nuclear-...

item.title: Blue Mountains highway closed for minimum three months due to major structural failure
item.description: The Great Western Highway through the Blue Mountains will remain closed for at least three months following a major geotechnical failure, raising significant concerns for residents and businesses in w...

item.title: LPG shortage in India disrupts weddings, food services, and daily life amid Iran conflict
item.description: The ongoing war in Iran has significantly disrupted LPG and oil supplies to India, impacting daily life across the country. Small-scale caterers, tiffin services, and restaurants are facing severe sho...

item.title: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla emphasises equality of rules for all members
item.description: In New Delhi, following the rejection of a no-confidence motion against him, Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla has resumed his official duties and firmly stated that the parliamentary rules are the same for ...

item.title: Nairobi County Offers One-Month Levy Waiver to Support Flood Recovery
item.description: Nairobi County Government has announced a one-month waiver on all levies and approvals normally required for residents to carry out repair and maintenance works on properties damaged by the recent hea...

item.title: Regina Explores Options for Library Renewal Project Amid Financial Constraints
item.description: The City of Regina and Regina Public Library (RPL) are actively seeking a feasible solution for a planned renewal project as the municipality confronts significant debt challenges. The project, aimed ...

item.title: Philippine Stock Market Outlook Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: The Philippine Stock Exchange (PSE) has experienced significant volatility following the US and Israel’s attack on Iran on February 28, 2026. The PSEi fell sharply to 6,006.22 on March 9, breaking bel...

item.title: Parole board rejects request for escorted family visits by convicted murderer Mark Twitchell
item.description: Mark Twitchell, a convicted murderer serving a life sentence in Alberta, was denied permission by the parole board for two short, escorted visits to see his parents. Twitchell, who murdered Johnny Alt...
